title: Home Renovation in Scarsdale: What You Need to Know Before You Start
excerpt: A Scarsdale home renovation means working with your local building department and finding the right architect and contractor to lead the project. Here's what homeowners should know.
content:
Why Scarsdale Homeowners Choose Design-Build

If you own a home in Scarsdale, you've probably thought about what's possible. Maybe your kitchen is stuck in the 1990s. Maybe you need a second bathroom. Maybe you're imagining a second story or a primary suite addition that actually feels like your own retreat.

The problem is that most homeowners don't know where to start. You could hire an architect, then hunt for a general contractor, then hope they both show up on the same page. Or you could work with a design-build firm where one person—a licensed architect in New York who is also a licensed general contractor—runs everything.

That's exactly how House Architecture & Construction, PLLC operates. Silvio M. Luca, the principal, is both. He designs the project and builds it. He pulls the permits. He manages the schedule. There's no gap between the vision and what actually gets built.

What Makes Scarsdale Projects Different

Scarsdale is a beautiful town with character. A lot of homes here were built in the early-to-mid twentieth century. They have bones. They have details. But they also have layouts that don't work anymore and systems that need updating.

Some homeowners in Scarsdale want to restore those historic details and make the home function for today. Others want to open up walls, add modern kitchens, and create spaces that actually work for how their families live now. Both approaches are legitimate. Both require someone who understands the town, the building codes, and how to work with the village building department.

Like every municipality in Westchester County, Scarsdale has its own permit process and building department. That's normal. It just means you need a residential architect in Scarsdale who knows the local rules and has relationships with the inspectors who review your work.

From Concept to Moving Day

A good home renovation in Westchester starts with listening. What do you actually want? Not what you think you should want. What would make your life better?

Then comes design. A licensed architect in Westchester NY should draw options, not just one rigid plan. You should see what the space will feel like. You should understand the cost implications of different choices.

Then comes building. A general contractor in Westchester who also holds your architectural vision can adapt when real conditions demand it. He can problem-solve with the homeowner and the design in mind, not just chase the lowest line item.

Whether you're planning a kitchen remodel, a bathroom renovation, home additions in Westchester, or a complete historic restoration, the process works best when one person owns the whole thing.

Ready to Start Your Scarsdale Home Renovation?

If you're ready to talk about what's possible for your Scarsdale home, call (914) 224-7383 for a free consultation. Silvio will listen to what you want and help you understand what it takes to build it right.
